description: Maps common symptoms/problems to relevant lessons for debugging Claude Code internals

symptoms.hint: Hook config is snapshot-captured at startup; restart Claude Code after changing settings.json, and verify the hook matcher (event type + pattern) is correct.
Check that your permission rules in settings.json use the correct tool name and pattern; rules are evaluated top-down with first-match-wins semantics.
Compaction triggers at ~80% of the context window; reduce system prompt size, split work across sessions, or use memory to persist critical facts across compactions.
Agents inherit the parent's permission config but get a fresh conversation; check that the task description is clear and that resource limits (max concurrent agents) are not exceeded.
MCP servers are started as child processes at boot; check the server command/args in settings.json, verify the binary exists, and look for stderr output in the MCP connection log.
Skills must have valid frontmatter with a name/description and be placed in a recognized skill directory (~/.claude/skills/ or project .claude/skills/); check the discovery path.
Settings use a cascade: enterprise MDM > organization > project > user; a higher-priority layer may be overriding your change. Restart Claude Code after editing settings.json.
Auto-memory writes to MEMORY.md files in .claude/ directories; check file permissions and verify memory extraction is enabled in settings (it runs at conversation end).
Coordinator mode requires the model to decide to dispatch; ensure your prompt clearly describes parallelizable subtasks and that max worker count is not set to zero.
Teams/swarm uses tmux panes and a file-based mailbox for inter-agent communication; verify tmux is installed, the session exists, and agents are polling their mailbox paths.
Bash commands pass through security validators that block destructive patterns (rm -rf /, etc.); check permission rules and sandbox settings for allowed command patterns.
File writes require read-before-write verification and respect permission rules; ensure the path is within the allowed workspace and that no deny rule matches the file pattern.
Sessions are stored as JSONL transcripts in ~/.claude/sessions/; use `claude --resume` with the session ID. Cloud sync may need to complete before a session appears on another machine.
The system prompt is assembled from CLAUDE.md, memory, tool definitions, and dynamic sections; large CLAUDE.md files or many MCP tools can push it past cache-friendly boundaries.
Telemetry respects the settings.json telemetry flag and environment variables; check that telemetry is not disabled by an enterprise MDM policy or CLAUDE_TELEMETRY_DISABLED env var.
Plugins are resolved from the marketplace manifest with dependency checks and version pinning; verify the plugin name, check the versioned cache, and look for resolution conflicts.
OAuth uses PKCE flow with tokens stored in the system keychain; check that the callback port is not in use, the keychain entry is not corrupted, and the token has not expired.
Git integration is filesystem-first with a background file watcher; ensure you are in a git repo, the watcher process is running, and .gitignore is not hiding relevant changes.

Compaction uses summarization to preserve key facts; context collapse (L69) is reversible via marble-origami persistence. Pin critical information in MEMORY.md or CLAUDE.md so it survives compaction, and keep individual messages focused.
Commands are registered at startup from built-in definitions, skills, and .claude/commands/ directories; verify the command file exists and has the correct format.
Tools come from built-in definitions, MCP servers, and plugins; check that the tool's source is configured and the MCP server providing it connected successfully at startup.
Boot time is dominated by MCP server startup and settings resolution; reduce the number of MCP servers or defer non-essential ones to cut cold-start latency.

The query engine has built-in retry with exponential backoff for transient API errors; persistent 429s mean you are hitting rate limits -- reduce parallel agents or wait. In v2.1.104+, partial streaming content is preserved on timeout instead of being discarded.

v2.1.104 added partial yield protection: if content blocks were already streamed to you, a timeout no longer discards them. Set CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONSTREAMING_FALLBACK to prevent any fallback. The default idle timeout is 90s (override via CLAUDE_STREAM_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS).
The Ink renderer uses Yoga for layout in the terminal; resize events, non-standard terminal emulators, or missing Unicode support can cause display issues.
Notifications use terminal-notifier or OSC escape sequences depending on platform; check that your terminal supports the notification method and it is not disabled in settings.
/setup-bedrock is only visible when CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK is set; it launches the interactive Bedrock configuration wizard.
/stop-hook is currently disabled (isEnabled: false in v2.1.92); use the full hooks system via settings.json or /hooks to configure Stop hooks.
CLAUDE_CODE_EXECPATH is automatically injected into every spawned shell/hook environment — use $CLAUDE_CODE_EXECPATH in hook scripts to invoke the claude binary without hard-coding a path.

Set CLAUDE_REMOTE_CONTROL_SESSION_NAME_PREFIX (or --session-name-prefix) to customize auto-generated remote control session names; defaults to the machine hostname, sanitized to lowercase alphanumeric.
/autofix-pr is gated behind remote sessions. In v2.1.94 it only enables when the Claude.ai subscriber gate passes and the allow_remote_sessions policy is enabled.
/team-onboarding is a built-in prompt command gated by CLAUDE_CODE_TEAM_ONBOARDING or a feature flag. It generates onboarding content from your usage data and writes ONBOARDING.md.

v2.1.94 adds a Mantle provider path. Set CLAUDE_CODE_USE_MANTLE to select it, ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_MANTLE_BASE_URL to override the default AWS-region-derived endpoint, and CLAUDE_CODE_SKIP_MANTLE_AUTH only for debugging.

Set CLAUDE_CODE_CERT_STORE to 'system' (OS certs), 'bundled' (Node.js built-in), or 'bundled,system' (both, the default). For corporate proxies with custom CAs, use 'system' or set NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS to your CA bundle path. Changes take effect on settings reload without restart.

Dynamic loops (tengu_kairos_loop_dynamic flag) auto-stop after recurringMaxAgeMs (default 7 days). Delays are clamped to [60, 3600] seconds. Check if the loop aged out via tengu_loop_dynamic_wakeup_aged_out telemetry.
When tengu_cinder_almanac is enabled, /loop offers cloud scheduling for intervals >= 60min or daily phrasing. Cloud schedules persist across sessions; local loops stop when the session closes.
Set CLAUDE_CODE_SDK_HAS_OAUTH_REFRESH when your SDK parent process provides a token refresh callback. On 401, Claude Code calls back to get a fresh token instead of failing.

/dream is gated by server-side GrowthBook flag tengu_kairos_dream (default: false). The server does not send this flag, so it falls through to the default. Workaround: use scripts/patch-dream.sh to inject the flag into the local cache with a watcher that survives the SDK cache writeback. Binary patching is not viable (code-signed Bun SEA, SIGKILL on macOS).

All local override mechanisms (env vars, config overrides) are stubbed/dead-code in the production binary. Flags are evaluated server-side via remoteEval. Binary is code-signed (SIGKILL on modification). For flags absent from server response, cache injection with a filesystem watcher can work. See L68 GrowthBook internals and scripts/patch-dream.sh for the technique.
/recap is gated behind tengu_sedge_lantern feature flag (default: false). Check with CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AWAY_SUMMARY=true to force-enable. Also check awaySummaryEnabled in /config settings.

Byte-level watchdog (CLAUDE_ENABLE_BYTE_WATCHDOG) detects transport-layer stalls. Event-level watchdog detects SSE parsing stalls. Set CLAUDE_ENABLE_BYTE_WATCHDOG=false to disable byte watchdog. Check tengu_stream_watchdog_default_on flag (default: true).
Multi-repo support uses CLAUDE_CODE_REPO_CHECKOUTS (JSON map of label→path) and CLAUDE_CODE_BASE_REFS (JSON map of label→ref). Both must be valid JSON with string→string entries.
/think-back and /thinkback-play were completely removed in v2.1.107. They are no longer available.

REPL mode is gated behind tengu_slate_harbor (default: false). Set CLAUDE_CODE_REPL=true to force-enable. Only works on cli/remote entrypoints. import/require are blocked — code runs in sealed VM. Tools are restricted to Read/Write/Edit/Bash/NotebookEdit via OkH set.
REPL VM state is compaction-aware — context compaction clears the VM state and warns. Variables persist within a session but not across compaction events. Check replay-log for state hydration.

Rate limit upgrade paths use a 3-layer system: server header hints (anthropic-ratelimit-unified-upgrade-paths), client lever hints (oV9 — gated by tengu_garnet_plover, pro+seven_day only), and interactive options menu. Early warnings fire at percentage-based thresholds before actual rejection.

Multi-repo checkout is CCR v2 infrastructure only — requires CLAUDE_CODE_USE_CCR_V2 to be enabled. Env vars are set by the external CCR orchestrator, not for local CLI use. Branch watching uses fs.watchFile on .git/HEAD at 1s intervals.

33 managed-agents docs (~324KB) are embedded in the binary as part of the claude-api built-in skill. Disabled by CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_CLAUDE_API_SKILL env var. Language detection via ZU5() selects docs by project language; Go/Java/Ruby/PHP/C# have no managed-agents specific files.

Remote Workflow Commands (/autopilot, /bugfix, /dashboard, /docs, /investigate) require CCR v2 ($X4() gate). They're hidden entirely when CCR v2 is not enabled. Requires CLAUDE_CODE_USE_CCR_V2 and server-side provisioning. All five spawn a remote session via POST /v1/sessions with beta header ccr-byoc-2025-07-29.

The Advisor Tool (server-side reviewer model) may intercept tool calls. Gates: first-party API only, opus-4-6/opus-4-7/sonnet-4-6 only, tengu_sage_compass2 flag on (or CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_EXPERIMENTAL_ADVISOR_TOOL=1). Hard-disable with CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_ADVISOR_TOOL=1.

PushNotification requires: user push setting enabled (not config_off), user not actively at terminal (not user_present), and Remote Control bridge running locally (not bridge_inactive). Check disabledReason in tool output. Only 'proactive' status is accepted. KAIROS is the server-side push service; bridge is the local relay.

Context Hint API lets the server signal compaction via HTTP 422/424/409/529 or SSE invalid_request with type=context_hint_rejection. Triggers keep-recent micro-compaction (sT9, keepRecent=5). Only activates on first-party API on repl_main_thread* when tengu_hazel_osprey is on. No env var disables it — feature-flag controlled.

Fullscreen precedence (first match wins): CLAUDE_CODE_NO_FLICKER=1 hard disables, CLAUDE_CODE_FULLSCREEN=1 hard enables, tmux -CC auto-disables, then userSettings.tui, then tengu_pewter_brook. /tui respawns the process via child_process.spawn. If terminal goes blank, your terminal may not support alt-screen — set CLAUDE_CODE_NO_FLICKER=1.

Proxy Auth Helper requires strict CLAUDE_CODE_PROXY_AUTHENTICATE="1" (literal string, not "true"). Shell command set in settings under proxyAuthHelper. Project/local settings need workspace trust. 30s exec timeout; falls back to stale cache on exec failure rather than failing the request.

pattern: system prompt not what I configured
symptoms.hint: In CCR/remote contexts, the server can replace the system prompt entirely via a GrowthBook feature. Requires both CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE truthy AND CLAUDE_CODE_SYSTEM_PROMPT_GB_FEATURE set to a valid GB feature name returning a non-empty string. Falls back to default on any failure. Distinct from L71's quiet_salted_ember section rename.

Slow first-byte watchdog logs 'warn' after CLAUDE_SLOW_FIRST_BYTE_MS (default 30000ms) with no stream chunk. Purely observational — does NOT abort the request. Complements L74's byte-level watchdog which does abort.
Unknown slash commands now trigger fuzzy-match suggestion via _a5() → BCH(). Writes 'unknown command "X"' + '└ Did you mean claude Y?' to stderr, emits tengu_unknown_command_suggestion, exits 1.

pattern: got a newer version than expected
symptoms.hint: Canary channel (rp1()) reads GrowthBook feature tengu_canary expecting {external: semver}. Native-installer target-picker op1() overrides target if canary > target AND !exceedsMaxVersion. Server-controlled rolling canary — not user-configurable.

pattern: why do my colleague and I get different claude behavior
symptoms.hint: v2.1.110-v2.1.111 moved significant behavior to the server side. Advisor Tool (tengu_sage_compass2), Context Hint API (tengu_hazel_osprey), System Prompt GB Override (CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE + user-supplied GB feature), Canary channel (tengu_canary), Fullscreen rollout (tengu_pewter_brook) all let Anthropic alter behavior per-user/per-org without a binary release. Two users on the same binary can get materially different experiences.

All five Remote Workflow Commands (/autopilot, /bugfix, /dashboard, /docs, /investigate) were removed outright in v2.1.113 — no feature flag, no deprecation shim, code deleted. See L85. The back-end CCR v2 infrastructure (L73) and /autofix-pr (L60) survive. L77 remains as historical documentation for what v2.1.110 shipped.

`/less-permission-prompts` was renamed to `/fewer-permission-prompts` in v2.1.113. The methodology prompt body is byte-identical; only the command name changed. The unknown-command did-you-mean helper (_a5()) should suggest the new name. See L85.

v2.1.113 added CLAUDE_ASYNC_AGENT_STALL_TIMEOUT_MS (default 600000ms / 10min). When no progress message arrives within the timeout, the async agent signal is aborted and tengu_async_agent_stall_timeout is emitted with agent_type/stall_ms/last_message_type/message_count. See L85.

v2.1.113 added CLAUDE_CODE_BS_AS_CTRL_BACKSPACE. Auto-enabled on win32 except mintty/cygwin. Set truthy to force-on, explicit false to force-off. Fixes the Windows quirk where backspace arrives as BS (\x08) instead of Ctrl+Backspace (\x7F). See L85.

v2.1.113 added CLAUDE_CODE_DECSTBM opt-in for ANSI Set Top/Bottom Margin escape sequences in the fullscreen TUI, plus tengu_marlin_porch as the GrowthBook rollout gate. Requires TTY + capability check + not-CI. Env var short-circuits the flag. See L85 for resolver details, L81 for fullscreen TUI context.

v2.1.113 added CLAUDE_BG_BACKEND. When exactly "daemon", the background stdout writer latches EIO/EPIPE into a flag (L_9) instead of throwing. Used to stabilize writes when the parent TTY has detached under a daemon supervisor. See L85.

v2.1.113 adds a 'Show message timestamps' setting (showMessageTimestamps, default false). Toggle is only visible in /config when GrowthBook flag tengu_silk_hinge is enabled — a server-gated UX rollout. Changes fire tengu_show_message_timestamps_setting_changed telemetry. See L85.

pattern: claude stays running after terminal close
symptoms.hint: v2.1.113 adds daemon-mode support via CLAUDE_BG_BACKEND=daemon: SIGHUP is ignored (logged SIGHUP_ignored_bg), stdout EIO/EPIPE is latched instead of thrown, and the 30s TTY-orphan detector is bypassed. Paired with a new user setting autoAddRemoteControlDaemonWorker (consumer is not present in the binary — likely server-side or forthcoming). Together these sketch a 'Claude Code as daemon worker under Remote Control' architecture. See L85 for semantics, L37 for Bridge/Remote context.

v2.1.113 adds MCP call watchdog (activeCallWatchdogs set on MCP transport state). Every 30s an in-flight MCP tool call logs 'Tool X still running (Ns elapsed)'. If the transport observed an error mid-call, the call is aborted 90s after the error mark with 'MCP server X transport dropped mid-call; response for tool Y was lost'. Closes a hole where MCP tool calls could hang indefinitely after transport errors. See L85.

v2.1.113 adds unclean-exit detection at startup. When a prior interactive session is found to have exited uncleanly, a startup message 'Prior session exited uncleanly: SID (vX)' is logged and tengu_unclean_exit fires with session_age_sec, prior_version, on_current_version. Pure observability — does not auto-resume. See L85.

/update is still isEnabled:()=>false and isHidden:true in v2.1.113 (not user-visible), but its implementation is being actively iterated. Two refusal paths now emit tengu_update_refused: active_tasks (refuses while any task is running/pending) and transcript_path_drift (refuses when session transcript is in a different project dir). Signals an upcoming staged launch of in-place native-installer upgrade. See L85, and L68 for the v2.1.101 state where /update was first introduced-but-disabled.

v2.1.113 adds tengu_image_resize_degraded telemetry emitted when an image block throws a VI error during processing; the block is substituted with a text placeholder '[Image could not be processed: <message>]'. No user-configurable gate — purely observational. See L85.

/fork is interactive-only (S8() non-interactive mode disables it) and gated behind two layers: set CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1 to force-enable, or wait for the tengu_copper_fox GB flag to flip on for your account (default false). /fork does not appear in `claude -p` headless sessions at all. See L87.

v2.1.118 folded /cost and /stats into /usage as aliases. They still work when typed (alias resolution at the command registry), but no longer show up as standalone commands in /help or autocomplete. In interactive mode you get the unified TUI dashboard ('Show session cost, plan usage, and activity stats'); in headless mode (claude -p) you get the brief 'cost and duration' string. See L88.

v2.1.117 removed /autocompact outright (both the command and its tengu_autocompact_* telemetry). Configure the auto-compact window size via /config settings UI or edit the underlying setting key in settings.json directly. See L87.

v2.1.117 removed /stop-hook entirely. It had been isEnabled:()=>false (shelf-parked) since v2.1.92 (L57). To configure Stop hooks, edit .claude/settings.json directly with a `hooks` section specifying event:'Stop'. See L87 and L10 (hooks system).

v2.1.117 landed directory-level locking (proper-lockfile) around WIF user-OAuth token refresh; v2.1.118 added all 5 telemetry events on top. The lock is on <config_dir>/credentials/ (the DIRECTORY, not the file), acquired via Gv_ → Vk4 → RY(path, {onCompromised: wH}). Retry budget is exactly tvq=5 attempts with 1000-2000ms jittered backoff = 5-10s max wait. On exhaustion: throws typed error e5 with message 'Could not acquire credentials lock at <path> after 5 retries'. If tengu_wif_user_oauth_lock_retry_limit fires, another Claude Code process is stuck holding the lock — investigate with `lsof <config_dir>/credentials/.lock` and `ls -la <config_dir>/credentials/.lock`. If confirmed orphaned, `rmdir <config_dir>/credentials/.lock` clears it (safe only when no live holder). tengu_oauth_401_recovered_from_disk indicates the post-hoc disk re-read recovery path succeeded — separate from the lock, catches cases where a sibling refreshed between in-memory cache load and the outbound request. See L88, and L38 for the base OAuth flow.

pattern: agent hook did not complete within 50 turns
symptoms.hint: Claude Code has a previously-undocumented hook TYPE: the AI verification hook (bs7). Instead of a shell command, an agent hook spawns a dedicated sub-conversation that returns structured output {ok: true} (allow stop) or {ok: false, reason} (block). v2.1.118 added tengu_agent_stop_hook_blocking telemetry for the !ok outcome — the mechanism itself pre-existed. Limits: max 50 turns (constant B=50), 60s default timeout (override via hookConfig.timeout in seconds), permissionMode:'dontAsk', thinking disabled. System prompt for Stop/SubagentStop events: 'You are verifying a stop condition in Claude Code. Your task is to verify that the agent completed the given plan.' Read(<transcript>) is auto-allowed. Five outcomes: success, blocking, cancelled (max turns), cancelled (no structured output), non_blocking_error. If the hook fires often and uses tool-call chains, expect latency — consider raising timeout or simplifying the verification prompt. See L88 for the full mechanism and L10 for the shell-command hook baseline.

/fork has three distinct execution paths with very different semantics. (1) User types /fork <directive>: backgrounded (isAsync:true) via _a7 → quH → xy, returns immediately, parent NOT blocked, shows '<emoji> forked <name> (<id4>)' system message, uses ph fork subagent type (tools:['*'], maxTurns:200, model:'inherit', permissionMode:'bubble'), gets full parent messages + REPL replay log. Guard: 'Cannot fork before the first conversation turn' (new in v2.1.117) if no renderedSystemPrompt. (2) Slash command with context:'fork' frontmatter: synchronous via V75, parent BLOCKS, inline progress streaming, uses H.agent from frontmatter or general-purpose fallback, returns <local-command-stdout>. (3) Skill invoked via Skill tool with context:'fork': synchronous via C75 (NEW in v2.1.118), same semantics as V75 but reaches via Skill tool path. See L87 for details. The 'fork' subagent type (ph) is ONLY used by path 1 — paths 2/3 use whatever agent is specified.

v2.1.118 added shadow validation (pjH) that calls qT1[kind]().strict().safeParse(frontmatter) and emits tengu_frontmatter_shadow_unknown_key / _mismatch telemetry on drift. There is NO formal primary schema — primary frontmatter handling is imperative (Cz8 reads properties directly and ignores unknown keys), so the Zod schemas added in v2.1.118 are the only formal validation. Per-session dedup via Gj9 Set means each unique (event,surface,detail) tuple fires once. Dispatch table has 3 entries: skill (eO1), agent (HT1), output-style (_T1) — custom commands are validated as 'skill'. The big known drift: progressMessage is documented in L11 but NOT in eO1, so any skill declaring it fires telemetry on load. Also watch kebab-vs-camel drift: schema uses allowed-tools / argument-hint / disable-model-invocation / user-invocable (kebab) for skills but tools / disallowedTools / mcpServers / maxTurns / initialPrompt / permissionMode (camel) for agents. See L88 for the full schema tables.

v2.1.118 dark-launched /pro-trial-expired (isEnabled:()=>false). Currently inert. When the date-gate or GB flag enables it, users whose Pro plan trial has ended will see an upsell/renewal UI with tracked choices (tengu_pro_trial_expired_choice). Combined with CLAUDE_CODE_SUBSCRIPTION_TYPE / CLAUDE_CODE_RATE_LIMIT_TIER env vars (L87), this is test-surface for the Pro plan rollout. See L88.
